summary: The third pharyngeal arch primarily gives rise to the stylopharygeus. Among listed options, only the tensor tympani is uniquely derived from a different source than other indicated muscles which come mainly from either fourth or sixth pharyngeal arches as shown by anatomical studies detailing muscular derivations precisely across various skeletal elements including facial bones and associated soft tissues via neural crest cell migration patterns during
